title: Research scheduler: recurring multi-model research jobs
description: A new src/research/ module that defines recurring research jobs (question + cadence + model-set + target repo/domain), fires them via a deterministic runner that uses fleet tier->provider routing for genuine model diversity, lands results as markdown research notes in the target vault plus Findings bubbled to the priority hub, gates all web/online research behind a new opt-in egress axis (default off, mirroring the medical egress pattern), and emits a morning digest artifact the Telegram bot can push as a silent scheduled message.

approvalEvidence: .bober/research/research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md#L99-L100 (scheduler net-new: thin research-job config + harness cron firing bober run/fleet/chat)
.bober/research/research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md#L181-L183,#L197 (online research = egress, scheduler-friendly weekly marker research)
src/fleet/tier-policy.ts#L50-L71 (DifficultyTier->provider/model block table for model diversity)
src/medical/egress.ts#L1-L60 + src/config/schema.ts#L374-L401 (EgressGuard / opt-in axis pattern to mirror)

clarificationQuestions.question: Which scheduling mechanism should fire recurring jobs: an in-repo loop, OS cron/launchd, or a harness scheduler?

options.label: A
options.description: Idempotent `bober research tick` that runs only due jobs, triggered externally by OS cron/launchd; plus optional `--watch` in-process loop.

options.label: C
options.description: Depend on a hosted/harness scheduler service.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — research doc says fire `bober run`/`fleet` on a cadence via harness-level cron (L99-L100) and warns hosted/OAuth schedulers are unfit for unattended runs (L135, L141). An idempotent tick is testable with an injected clock and stays connector-agnostic.

clarificationQuestions.question: Where should research-job definitions live: a config section, a JSON file store, or the FactStore?

options.label: A
options.description: JSON file store under .bober/research/jobs/<jobId>.json — mutable user data, mirrors other .bober/ artifact stores.

options.label: C
options.description: Rows in the FactStore SQLite db.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — jobs are mutable user data, not project config; the research doc calls it a 'thin research job config' (L100) and the repo already keeps mutable artifacts under .bober/ (specs, contracts, memory).

clarificationQuestions.question: How should run results bubble to the priority hub, given the hub spec is a sibling dependency built first?

options.label: A
options.description: Write a markdown research note to the target vault (owned here) AND emit a Finding via the hub's Finding write surface from spec-20260628-priority-hub.

options.label: B
options.description: Only write a note; let the hub scrape vault notes.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — dependsOn=spec-20260628-priority-hub guarantees the hub Finding API exists at build time. Finding shape is locked in research doc L121-L126. The note is the self-owned, independently verifiable artifact; the Finding is the hub hand-off.

clarificationQuestions.question: For model diversity, should a job use the full FleetCoordinator (spawns child agent-bober runs) or a lightweight multi-provider query loop over tier-policy blocks?

options.label: A
options.description: Lightweight loop resolving distinct provider/model blocks from src/fleet/tier-policy.ts and querying each for the research question.

options.label: B
options.description: Full FleetCoordinator spawning isolated child runs per model.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — FleetCoordinator (src/fleet/coordinator.ts) spawns full child agent-bober runs, far heavier than answering a research question. Reusing the tier-policy table (tier-policy.ts:50-71) gives provider/model diversity directly. Research doc L80 confirms the judging substrate is already internal.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should a job whose target domain is medical invoke the medical analysis pass instead of the generic multi-model query?

options.label: A
options.description: Runner exposes a pluggable domain-analyzer interface with a generic multi-model default; medical-analysis can register later without a hard dependency.

options.label: B
options.description: Hard-wire a medical branch now, depending on spec-20260628-medical-analysis.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — spec-20260628-medical-analysis is a sibling, NOT in this spec's dependsOn, so a hard wire would create an undeclared coupling. A pluggable analyzer keeps the runner domain-agnostic (research doc L181-L183) while letting the medical pass register when it lands.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Autonomous self-approval. Design approved against locked research doc and substrate evidence: .bober/research/research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md (L99-L100 scheduler, L121-L126 Finding, L181-L183/L197 online-research egress), src/fleet/tier-policy.ts:50-71, src/medical/egress.ts:1-60, src/config/schema.ts:374-401, src/cli/commands/medical.ts:130-185, src/state/facts.ts:20-33.

A — canonical idempotent `bober research tick` (runs only jobs whose nextDueAt <= injected now), plus optional `--watch` in-process loop; OS cron/launchd documented as the recommended unattended trigger. Evidence: research doc L99-L100 (cadence firing), L135/L141 (hosted OAuth unfit unattended).
A — JSON file store at .bober/research/jobs/<jobId>.json. Evidence: research doc L100 ('thin research job config'); .bober/ already holds mutable artifact stores (specs/, contracts/, memory/).

A — write a vault markdown note (self-owned) AND emit a Finding via the hub's write surface (spec-20260628-priority-hub, declared dependsOn). Finding fields per research doc L121-L126. Evidence: research doc L96-L97, L121-L126.

A — lightweight multi-provider query loop resolving distinct provider/model blocks from src/fleet/tier-policy.ts:50-71; not the full FleetCoordinator. Evidence: src/fleet/coordinator.ts (spawns child runs), tier-policy.ts:50-71, research doc L80.

A — pluggable domain-analyzer interface with a generic multi-model default; medical-analysis registers later (not a declared dependency). Evidence: research doc L181-L183; briefing scope ('this CALLS them'); spec-20260628-medical-analysis absent from dependsOn.

assumptions: Scheduling is an idempotent `bober research tick` (runs only due jobs, injected clock) + optional `--watch`, with OS cron/launchd as the recommended unattended trigger. Evidence: research doc L99-L100, L135, L141.
Research jobs are stored as JSON files under .bober/research/jobs/<jobId>.json (mutable user data, not bober.config.json). Evidence: research doc L100; .bober/ artifact-store convention.

Run output is a markdown research note in the target vault PLUS a Finding emitted through the hub's write surface (spec-20260628-priority-hub). Finding fields per research doc L121-L126. Evidence: research doc L96-L97, L121-L126.

Model diversity comes from a lightweight loop over distinct provider/model blocks resolved from src/fleet/tier-policy.ts:50-71, not from FleetCoordinator child-run spawning. Evidence: tier-policy.ts:50-71, src/fleet/coordinator.ts.

The new online-research egress axis mirrors src/medical/egress.ts EgressGuard exactly: default false, code-enforced, assertAllowed throws when off. Evidence: src/medical/egress.ts:1-60, src/config/schema.ts:374-401.

The runner exposes a pluggable domain-analyzer interface (generic default) so the medical analysis pass can register later without an undeclared dependency. Evidence: research doc L181-L183; spec-20260628-medical-analysis not in dependsOn.

All new work is additive: existing fleet, medical, and run/pipeline behavior stays byte-identical when no research job runs. Evidence: additive-module convention used by src/medical/ and src/fleet/ phases.
outOfScope: The hub's cross-repo collection and priority ranking logic (owned by spec-20260628-priority-hub).
Calendar planning / free-busy slot-fill (owned by the calendar sibling spec).
Telegram bot transport, polling, whitelist, inline buttons, and message rendering (owned by the telegram sibling spec) — this spec only produces the digest content artifact.
Medical analysis internals: red-flag short-circuit, recommendation judge-loop, grounded synthesis (owned by spec-20260628-medical-analysis; this spec only invokes a registered analyzer).
Lab-PDF ingestion, SOPS encryption, and Obsidian MCP wiring (owned by other sibling specs).
Building a new model router/proxy (LiteLLM/OpenRouter) — model diversity reuses existing fleet tier-policy.

features.title: Research job definition and management
features.description: A research-job schema (question, cadence, model-set/tier, target repo/domain, online-research opt-in flag) with a JSON file store and a `bober research job add|list|remove` CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: A ResearchJob zod schema validates the fields {question, cadence, tier/modelSet, targetRepo, domain, onlineResearch} and rejects an empty question.
AC2: `bober research job add` persists a JSON file under .bober/research/jobs/<jobId>.json and `bober research job list` prints the stored job's id, question, and cadence.

features.title: Multi-model research run with model diversity
features.description: A deterministic runner that executes one stored job by querying at least two distinct provider/model blocks resolved from fleet tier-policy, synthesizes the answers into a markdown research note written to the target vault, and emits a Finding to the hub.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Running a job resolves at least two distinct provider/model blocks from src/fleet/tier-policy.ts and the produced note records which model produced each contribution.
AC2: The run writes a markdown research note with frontmatter (job id, question, models, generatedAt) to the target vault and emits one Finding via the hub Finding surface.

features.title: Online-research egress axis (opt-in, default off)
features.description: A new code-enforced egress axis for online/web research mirroring the medical EgressGuard pattern; default false, fail-closed. The runner's web-retrieval step only executes when the axis is opted in.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: The online-research egress axis defaults to false; the guard's assertAllowed throws when the axis is off and returns void when opted in via config.
AC2: With the axis off (default), a run performs zero outbound web requests; with the axis on, the run performs retrieval and the note cites the retrieved sources.

features.title: Recurring scheduler / cadence runner
features.description: Cadence evaluation (compute nextDueAt from a job's cadence using an injected clock) and an idempotent `bober research tick` that runs only due jobs and advances their nextDueAt, plus an optional `--watch` loop.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober research tick` with an injected now runs only jobs whose nextDueAt <= now and leaves not-yet-due jobs untouched.
AC2: After a job runs, its nextDueAt advances by its cadence and lastRunAt is recorded; a second tick at the same now runs nothing.

features.title: Morning digest artifact
features.description: A digest builder that aggregates research notes/Findings produced within a time window into a digest artifact (markdown + JSON) the Telegram bot can push as a silent scheduled message; transport is out of scope.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober research digest --since <iso>` writes .bober/research/digests/<date>.{md,json} listing each job run in the window with title and top finding.
AC2: An empty window produces a digest stating no new research was produced (not an error or empty file).

integrationPoints: spec-20260628-priority-hub Finding write surface (results bubble up)
src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts (bober run / fleet entry, for reference)
techNotes.dataModel: ResearchJob {id, question, cadence, tier|modelSet, targetRepo, domain, onlineResearch, lastRunAt?, nextDueAt?} stored as JSON at .bober/research/jobs/<jobId>.json. Run output = vault markdown note (frontmatter) + hub Finding (schema per research doc L121-L126).
securityConsiderations: Online research is egress: gated behind a new opt-in axis defaulting to false (fail-closed), mirroring src/medical/egress.ts.
Hosted-OAuth connectors are unfit for unattended runs — prefer local-token/.ics paths where relevant (research doc L135, L141).
Digest content is non-sensitive summary only; raw sensitive detail must not be embedded (Telegram is not E2E-encrypted — research doc L141).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Online/web research performs zero outbound bytes unless the online-research egress axis is explicitly opted in via config.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test asserting the guard defaults to false and a default-config run issues no network call (injected fetch spy records zero calls).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Cadence and scheduling logic never reads the wall clock directly; the current time is injected so runs are deterministic and testable.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test passing two fixed `now` values and asserting deterministic due/not-due selection.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All work is additive — existing fleet, medical, and run/pipeline code paths are byte-identical when no research job is defined or run.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: build plus the existing vitest suite remaining green.

constraints: New code lives under src/research/ and src/cli/commands/research.ts; do not modify src/fleet/, src/medical/, or src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts behavior.
Reuse src/fleet/tier-policy.ts for model diversity; do not introduce a new model router (LiteLLM/OpenRouter).
Mirror src/medical/egress.ts for the egress axis; do not invent a different egress mechanism.
Stay within sprint.maxSprints=10 and sprintSize=medium (3-8 files per sprint).
